purpose: Rules every AI-assisted agent must follow to avoid hallucination, unsupported claims, and unsafe automation.

global_guardrails.name: Use only supplied evidence
global_guardrails.requirement: Agents must reason only from the input payload, MotherDuck-derived records, rule_catalog.json, and explicitly supplied sheet rows. Do not invent contracts, customers, bank products, risk rules, dates, amounts, or policies.

global_guardrails.name: Cite field-level evidence
global_guardrails.requirement: Every material conclusion must cite evidence_used as field=value strings, for example cashflow_gap_flag=true or 04_CONTRACTS.payment_terms=Possible LC/trade finance.

global_guardrails.name: Read rule catalog before decision
global_guardrails.requirement: Before outputting a risk, financing, partner, or decision result, agents must map their conclusion to one or more rule_id values from rule_catalog.json or sheet rule IDs from 13_RISK_RULES.

global_guardrails.name: Double-check output against original fields
global_guardrails.requirement: Before final JSON output, agents must verify that selected rule IDs and recommendations are consistent with source fields. If a conclusion depends on a missing/ambiguous field, list it in missing_info instead of guessing.

global_guardrails.name: No hidden final authority
global_guardrails.requirement: Agents never finalize contracts or external releases. They only recommend. Founder/HITL approval is required for final approval and external release.

global_guardrails.name: Conservative uncertainty handling
global_guardrails.requirement: When evidence is incomplete, conflicting, or low confidence, choose the safer interpretation: Need Clarification, Conditional Accept, Reject, or require Founder review. Do not upgrade to Accept without strong evidence.

global_guardrails.name: Structured JSON only
global_guardrails.requirement: AI-assisted agent outputs must be valid JSON matching the expected schema. Do not return markdown, prose-only answers, or chain-of-thought.

global_guardrails.name: No private chain-of-thought
global_guardrails.requirement: Do not expose private chain-of-thought. Provide concise reasoning_summary, rule_interpretation, evidence_used, tradeoffs, and missing_info.

global_guardrails.name: Python guardrail remains authoritative
global_guardrails.requirement: If AI output conflicts with deterministic mandatory flags, source data, or hard safety constraints, Python validation must keep or downgrade to the safer result.

Data & Finance Agent: Build Opportunity Profile from MotherDuck contract, customer, order, invoice, transaction, and cashflow records.
Run AI/NLP as an internal tool only after the finance evidence payload is compact.
Extract text signals from contract description, payment_terms, and delivery_note.
Do not override numeric fields unless evidence exists in input text or data.
If provider call fails, mark ai_used=false and use fallback reasoning.
Risk & Compliance Agent: Read risk_rule_catalog and 13_RISK_RULES rows before selecting risk rules.
Do not invent rule IDs. Only use rule IDs present in 13_RISK_RULES or rule_catalog.json.
If cashflow_gap_flag=true, RR-002 must remain selected even if AI omits it.
If low_margin_flag=true, RR-003 must remain selected even if AI omits it.
Decision & Partner Agent: Read financing, credit profile, bank product, risk output, and rule_catalog before recommending.
Do not recommend Accept when deterministic guardrail says Conditional Accept or Reject.
AI may make the recommendation more conservative, but Python must block unsafe upgrades.
Founder final approval remains required even when recommendation is Accept.
Orchestrator: Stop at every HITL gate until human action is supplied.
Do not trigger external release unless Founder final approval is Approved and external_release_gate_required=true.
